title: apocynaceae
intro: The Apocynaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as dogbane or oleander families. This family comprises over 4,300 species of plants, many of which are known for their toxic compounds and striking appearances. Apocynaceae includes a wide range of plants, from small shrubs to large trees, and can be found in various habitats around the world. These plants often feature showy flowers, milky sap, and sometimes, striking adaptations to attract pollinators.
